title: Micro-founded measurement of regional competitiveness in Europe (chapter)
outlet: Bruegel Blueprint #24 — Measuring competitiveness in Europe
summary_en: Chapter in Blueprint XXIV. Builds a micro-founded measure of regional competitiveness in Europe by aggregating firm-level productivity data within NUTS-2 regions. Shows that headline regional rankings move substantially when using micro-foundations vs. simple GVA-per-worker, and identifies which Hungarian and CEE regions are systematically over- or under-rated by the standard approach.
policy_relevance: Direct input for EU Cohesion Policy targeting and for the Hungarian RPM (regional development) strategy. The micro-founded ranking would change which regions appear 'competitive' on the EU dashboard, with consequences for how Cohesion funds are allocated. Caveat: depends on the availability of harmonised firm data — Hungarian regions are well-covered, but smaller member states are not.
